description: Per-audience rewrite specifications. The agent must produce three distinct rewrites of the source memo, each tailored to its named audience.

ceo_brief.purpose: 5-minute board-prep read. CEO needs to know: what happened, who was impacted, what we're doing, what the board-level signal is. NOT detailed technical root-cause.
must_include: the one-line summary of the outage
scope of impact (number of accounts, money/revenue framing)
the three or four most material remediations
ceo_brief.tone: Concise, executive. Direct stance. No hedging.

customer_email.purpose: Build trust. Explain what happened in human terms. Acknowledge the impact. Outline what we're doing differently. Offer the SLA credit explicitly.
must_include: clear acknowledgement of the outage and its scope
what we know about root cause (in plain language)
what we're committing to differently going forward
explicit reference to the SLA credit / how to request
a contact path (CSM, support, named exec)
must_not_include: internal candor about previous board signals or unaddressed warnings
the 'second outage in 90 days' framing — that's an internal signal, not a customer-facing one
customer_email.tone: Warm, direct, accountable. Active voice. No corporate hedging ('out of an abundance of caution', 'unforeseen circumstances').

legal_summary.audience: In-house counsel reviewing SLA credit obligations
legal_summary.purpose: Precise, dispassionate enumeration of facts relevant to SLA-credit determinations. Counsel uses this to advise finance on credit processing.
must_include: exact incident time window with timezone
the contractual SLA threshold (99.9% monthly) and whether it was nominally breached
the four cases of known downstream-automation harm
a recommendation on credit-request handling (case-by-case for relationship-driven, contractual for SLA-driven)
legal_summary.tone: Precise, factual, dispassionate. Use 'incident' not 'outage'. No apologies, no soft-pedaling.

audience_fit_per_rewrite.description: Does the rewrite match the target audience's information needs and style?

faithfulness_to_source.description: Does the rewrite preserve the substantive facts in the draft? No invented numbers, no contradictions.

no_inappropriate_content_per_rewrite.description: Does the rewrite avoid the must_not_include items? The most likely failure: customer email mentions the named engineer or includes the 'second outage in 90 days' board signal.

within_word_limit.description: Each rewrite within its specified max_words.

fabrication_count.description: Count of any facts in the rewrites not present in the source memo.
